steps.title: Introduce the variation details
steps.content: The first part, the number of jams available for tasting varied, is a complete clause. The following words explain how the number varied: some shoppers had twenty-four options and others had six.

steps.title: Reject comma and while errors
steps.content: Choice B creates a comma splice between complete clauses. Choice C adds while after a comma, but the second part explains variation rather than contrasting events. Choice D has the same illogical while relationship. A colon correctly introduces the explanation after the complete clause. The answer is A.
